title: cyathea pendula
intro: Cyathea pendula, also known as the Rattlesnake Fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyatheaceae. It is native to Australia and is a type of tree fern that is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cyathea pendula can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to support respiratory health, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
